_metadata.description: Controlled vocabulary for the 7 clinical study-type tiers used by backed_clinical_studies. Determines base points before evidence-level multipliers are applied.
_metadata.purpose: Single source of truth for study-type labels, explanations, and base scoring points in clinical evidence records.
_metadata.alignment: Pipeline source: scripts/data/backed_clinical_studies.json study_type field. Base points must match scripts/config/scoring_config.json section_C_evidence_research.study_type_base_points.

field_contract.id: Stable snake_case identifier found in backed_clinical_studies.study_type.
field_contract.name: User-facing label.
field_contract.notes: ≤200-char plain-English description shown in tap-to-learn modal.
field_contract.base_points: Base evidence points before evidence_level multiplier is applied.

study_types.name: Systematic review or meta-analysis
study_types.notes: Evidence synthesis across multiple studies. Highest base tier because it summarizes a broader body of clinical literature.

study_types.name: Multiple randomized trials
study_types.notes: Two or more randomized controlled trials. Strong direct evidence, especially when results point in the same direction.

study_types.name: Single randomized trial
study_types.notes: One randomized controlled trial. Useful direct evidence, but less robust than repeated trials or pooled analyses.

study_types.name: Clinical probiotic strain study
study_types.notes: Clinical evidence tied to a specific probiotic strain. Strain identity is essential for applying the finding.

study_types.name: Observational human study
study_types.notes: Human data without randomized assignment. Helpful for associations, but weaker for proving supplement effect.

study_types.name: Animal study
study_types.notes: Preclinical animal evidence. Supports plausibility but does not establish expected human benefit.

study_types.name: In-vitro study
study_types.notes: Cell, tissue, or laboratory evidence only. Useful for mechanism context, not direct clinical benefit.
